semanticCore: Transgression, going too far, injustice, remote, edge, or excess
quranicRelevance: Speaking words of extreme transgression (Shatata - 'ithan shatatan'), and judging without injustice ('la tushtit').

keyDerivations.meaning: Transgression, extreme excess, injustice, or outrageous lie

keyDerivations.form: Form IV Verb (active part/verb: la tushtit)
keyDerivations.meaning: To exceed limits, act unjustly, transgress, or go too far

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَنَا بِالْحَقِّ وَلَا تُشْطِطْ (38:22): So judge between us with truth, and do not transgress/exceed limits (the angels' plea to David).
summaryEn: The root represents extreme transgression (Shatat), condemning the pagans' outrageous lies about God and demanding absolute justice without transgression.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر التجاوز الفادح للحدود والعدل والافتراء الشديد (شطط)، واصفاً قول الكذب الفظيع (شططاً)، ومطالباً داود بعدم الجور (ولا تشطط).
rawText: أَشْطَطَ 4 see 1, in nine places. إِشْتَطَطَ 8 see 1, in two places. شاطّهُ 3 , (K,) inf. n. مُشَاطَّةٌ, (TA,) He vied with him in اِشْتِطَاط [i. e. going far, or beyond the due bounds, in offering a thing for sale and demanding a price for it, or in bargaining for a thing; or acting unjustly, wrongfully, injuriously, or tyrannically, in judging, &c.]. (K.) You say, شاطّهُ 🡻فَشَطَّهُ [He vied with him in so doing, and surpassed him, or overcame him, therein]. (TA.) See also 1, in the latter half of the paragraph. شطّط 2 , inf. n. تَشْطِيطٌ, He strove, laboured, exerted himself, or did his utmost, in acting unjustly, wrongfully, injuriously, or tyrannically, and exceeding the due bounds. (K, * TA.) See also 1, in the latter half of the paragraph. شَاطٌّ Anything Distant, remote, or far. (TA.) === A man whose two extremities are far from each other. (K.) === جَارِيَةٌ شَاطَّةٌ, (S, K,) and 🡻شَطَّةٌ, (K,) A girl tall, and of beautiful stature: (K:) or of just stature. (S, K.) ___ إِنَّكَ لَشَاطِّى Verily thou art acting unjustly, wrongfully, injuriously, or tyrannically, towards me in judging: occurring in a trad. (A'Obeyd, Az, S.) شَطَاطٌ Distance; remoteness; (S, K, TA;) as also 🡻شِطَاطٌ and 🡻شِطَّةٌ, with kesr, (K,) and 🡻مَشَطَّةٌ. (TA.) It is said in a trad., اَللّٰهُمَّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ وَعْثَآءِ السَّفَرِ وَكَآبَةِ 🡻الشِّطَّةِ وَسُوْءِ المُنْقَلَبِ i. e. [O God, verily I seek protection by Thee from trouble of travel, and grievousness] of distance of the space to be journeyed over, [and evilness of return to my home.] (TA.) === And Distance, or farness, between the two extremities of a man; as also 🡻شِطَاطٌ and 🡻شَطَاطَةٌ. (K.) And Tallness, and beauty of stature: (K:) or justness of stature: (IDrd, S, K:) as also 🡻شِطَاطٌ, (S, K,) in either sense. (K.) ___ Also, and 🡻شِطَاطٌ, Fragments of baked bricks. (K.) === [Freytag erroneously assigns the first and last meanings in this paragraph to شَطَّةٌ: and he adds, from Reiske, a meaning belonging to شِظَاظٌ.] شَطَاطَةٌ : see شَطَاطٌ. شَطَطٌ , for أَمْرٌ ذُو شَطَطٍ, (Mgh,) and قَوْلٌ ذُو شَطَطٍ, (Bd in lxxii. 4,) An action, or affair, (Mgh,) and a saying, (Bd,) that is extravagant, or exorbitant, or exceeding the due bounds. (Mgh, Bd.) [See 1, of which it is an inf. n.] شَطَوْطَى : see شَطُوطٌ. شَطُوطٌ A she-camel having a large hump; (As, S, K;) as also 🡻شَطَوْطَى: (K:) or large in the two sides of the hump: (TA:) pl. شَطَائِطُ. (K.) شَطٌّ The bank, or side, of a river: (S, Msb, K:) and of a valley: (S, Msb:) or, of the latter, the rising ground next the bottom: (AHn:) pl. شُطُوطٌ (Msb, K) and شُطَّانٌ; (K;) the latter occurring in a verse, accord. to one relation; but accord. to another relation, it is شُطْآنٌ, pl. of شَاطِئٌ, which is syn. with شَطٌّ. (TA.) === ‡ The side of a camel's hump; (S, K;) any side thereof: (S:) or the half thereof: (K:) pl. شُطُوطٌ. (S, K.) شَطَّةٌ : see شَاطٌّ. شَطْشَاطٌ A certain bird: (K:) IDrd says that it is asserted to have this meaning; but is not of established authority. (O.) شِطَاطٌ : see شَطَاطٌ, in four places. شِطَّةٌ : see شَطَاطٌ, in two places. مَشَطَّةٌ : see شَطَاطٌ. === Also Difficulty, distress, affliction, trouble, or inconvenience. (TA.)
